zh-tw.title: 土味情話大全
zh-tw.description: 讓人又甜又尷尬的土味情話合集，撩人必備語錄

en.title: Cheesy Pickup Lines
en.description: A collection of cheesy yet charming pickup lines guaranteed to make them smile (or cringe)

zh-tw.content:
「你知道你跟星星有什麼不一樣嗎？」
「什麼？」
「星星在天上，你在我心裡。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 土味情話入門款，成功率中等
variations: 你知道你跟月亮有什麼不一樣嗎？月亮會變，我對你的心不會變

en.content:
"Do you know what's different about you and the stars?"
"What?"
"The stars are in the sky, but you're in my heart."
en.editorNote: Classic starter, moderate success rate
variations: What's different about you and the moon? The moon changes, but my feelings for you don't.

zh-tw.content:
「我覺得你好像一道數學題。」
「為什麼？」
「因為我對你完全沒有解。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 學生族群特愛這類型
variations: 你就像微積分，讓我越算越上頭

en.content:
"I think you're like a math problem."
"Why?"
"Because I have absolutely no solution for you."
en.editorNote: Students love this type
variations: You're like calculus - the more I work on you, the more obsessed I get.

zh-tw.content:
「你喜歡喝水嗎？」
「喜歡啊。」
「那你已經喜歡上七十幾percent的我了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 理科生的撩人方式
variations: 你喜歡空氣嗎？喜歡啊。那你每一秒都在需要我了。

en.content:
"Do you like drinking water?"
"Sure."
"Then you already like about 70% of me."
en.editorNote: Science nerds' way of flirting
variations: Do you like breathing? Good — because I take your breath away, which means you need me to function.

zh-tw.content:
「你今天特別好看。」
「今天？那昨天呢？」
「昨天也是，明天也會是，每天都是。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 回答得很圓滑的示範
variations: 「你今天氣色很好。」「我昨天睡得不好耶。」「那你明天睡飽一定更好看，我現在就期待了。」

en.content:
"You look especially great today."
"Today? What about yesterday?"
"Yesterday too. And tomorrow. And every day."
en.editorNote: Smooth recovery example
variations: "You look great today." "I actually didn't sleep well last night." "Then tomorrow is going to be dangerous. I'm already worried for myself."

zh-tw.content:
你知道我最大的缺點是什麼嗎？

就是缺點你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 簡短有力的經典款
variations: 我有個缺點想改，就是缺少你的聯絡方式

en.content:
Do you know what my biggest shortcoming is?

Falling short of being with you.
en.editorNote: Short and punchy classic
variations: I have a flaw I'd like to fix: not having your number.

zh-tw.content:
「你是甜甜圈嗎？」
「為什麼？」
「因為我的生活遇到你，才算圓滿了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 甜食愛好者最適合用這招，對方很難不笑
variations: 你是珍珠奶茶嗎？因為沒有你，生活就少了那個讓人開心的驚喜

en.content:
"Are you a donut?"
"Why?"
"Because my life finally feels complete now that you're in it."
en.editorNote: Works best with someone who has a sweet tooth — hard to keep a straight face
variations: Are you boba tea? Because without you, my day is missing that little surprise that makes it worth it.

zh-tw.content:
「你是螢幕保護程式嗎？」
「為什麼這樣問？」
「因為自從遇到你，我就捨不得鎖畫面了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給愛滑手機的對象，效果加分
variations: 你是我手機的省電模式嗎？因為有你在，我什麼都不想做，只想看著你

en.content:
"Are you my screen saver?"
"What do you mean?"
"Because ever since I met you, I can't bring myself to lock the screen."
en.editorNote: Send this to someone who's always on their phone — extra points for irony
variations: Are you my low battery mode? Because with you around, I don't want to do anything else — just stay right here.

zh-tw.content:
「你知道你跟拼圖有什麼共同點嗎？」
「什麼？」
「沒有你，我這輩子都拼不完整。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 喜歡玩拼圖的對象超適用，也可以直接配上一張拼圖照片一起傳
variations: 你是我最後一塊拼圖——找了好久，原來你一直在這裡

en.content:
"Do you know what you have in common with a puzzle?"
"What?"
"Without you, I'll never be complete."
en.editorNote: Bonus points if you send this with a photo of a puzzle missing one piece
variations: You're my last puzzle piece — I searched everywhere, and you were here all along.

zh-tw.content:
「你信不信，只要你說一句話，我就知道你現在在想什麼？」
「好，你說說看。」
「你現在在想：『這個人怎麼這麼煩』，但你其實有一點點喜歡這種感覺。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 互動式情話，適合曖昧期試水溫，對方笑了就代表有戲
variations: 也可以用：「你現在在想——『這個人真的很無聊，但不知道為什麼我還是在這裡』」

en.content:
"Bet I know exactly what you're thinking right now."
"Oh yeah? Let's hear it."
"You're thinking: 'This person is so annoying.' But somewhere in there, you kind of enjoy it."
en.editorNote: Great for the flirty testing phase — if they laugh, that's a green light
variations: Alternate version: 'You're thinking: this person is ridiculous. And yet here you are, still reading this.'

zh-tw.content:
「你知道流星跟你有什麼共同點嗎？」
「什麼？」
「看到你的瞬間，讓人忍不住想許願。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 晚上看星星的時候說，效果翻倍，對方基本上沒有辦法不笑
variations: 你比流星還稀有——流星至少每年有固定的日子，但像你這樣的人，這輩子只遇得到一次

en.content:
"Do you know what you have in common with a shooting star?"
"What?"
"The moment I see you, I can't help but make a wish."
en.editorNote: Say this while stargazing for double effect — nearly impossible not to smile at
variations: You're rarer than a shooting star — they come around every year, but someone like you only happens once in a lifetime.

zh-tw.content:
「你喜歡吃棉花糖嗎？」
「還好啊。」
「那就是你的感覺——甜但不膩，讓人嚐一口就還想再一口。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 在甜點店或市集裡說，自然又不尷尬，對方多半會假裝翻白眼但心裡很開心
variations: 你比棉花糖還甜，但比它更難忘——棉花糖吃完就沒了，你讓我一直在想

en.content:
"Do you like cotton candy?"
"It's okay, I guess."
"That's exactly what you're like — sweet without being overwhelming. One taste and I always want more."
en.editorNote: Works best at a market or dessert shop — casual enough not to feel rehearsed
variations: You're sweeter than cotton candy, but way harder to forget — candy disappears, but you stay on my mind.

zh-tw.content:
「你是我的飛航模式嗎？」
「什麼意思？」
「因為只要跟你在一起，我就想把整個世界的訊號都關掉，只留下你。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給習慣低頭滑手機的對象，暗示你想要對方的全部注意力
variations: 你是我的勿擾模式——不是因為你讓我煩，是因為有你在，其他的通知都不重要了

en.content:
"Are you airplane mode?"
"What do you mean?"
"Because whenever I'm with you, I want to turn off every signal in the world and keep only you."
en.editorNote: Send to someone who's always on their phone — a gentle way of asking for their full attention
variations: You're my do-not-disturb mode — not because you're annoying, but because when you're here, nothing else needs my attention.

zh-tw.content:
「如果你今天找不到路，往我這個方向走就好。」
「你知道路嗎？」
「不知道。但我知道我在這裡等你，不管你從哪個方向來。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給迷路（字面意義或心情上）的另一半，安心感十足
variations: 我不確定前面的路怎麼走，但我確定我想跟你一起走過去

en.content:
"If you ever feel lost today, just walk toward me."
"Do you know the way?"
"No. But I know I'll be here waiting, no matter which direction you come from."
en.editorNote: Send this when your partner is literally lost, or just having an overwhelming day — works for both
variations: I don't know what's ahead of us. But I know I want to walk there with you.

zh-tw.content:
「你是美式咖啡嗎？」
「為什麼這樣問？」
「因為不管我加幾顆糖，你永遠讓我想再多喝一口。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 在咖啡廳說效果最好，或是傳給喜歡喝咖啡的人，附上一杯咖啡的照片更加分
variations: 你是拿鐵嗎？因為有你在，生活的苦都變得順口多了

en.content:
"Are you an Americano?"
"Why do you ask?"
"Because no matter how much sweetness I add to my life, I always want another cup of you."
en.editorNote: Works best at a coffee shop, or send with a photo of a coffee cup for extra points
variations: Are you a latte? Because with you around, even the bitter parts of life go down smooth.

zh-tw.content:
「你知道你跟Google地圖有什麼共同點嗎？」
「什麼？」
「每次我不知道該往哪裡走，只要想到你，我就知道方向了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給在人生路口感到迷惘的對象，或是在旅途中傳，讓對方秒懂你的心意
variations: 你是我的導航——不是因為我迷路，是因為有你在的方向，我都想去

en.content:
"Do you know what you have in common with Google Maps?"
"What?"
"Every time I don't know where I'm going, just thinking about you tells me exactly which direction to walk."
en.editorNote: Send while traveling together, or when your partner is at a crossroads in life
variations: You're my GPS — not because I'm lost, but because wherever you are is exactly where I want to go.

zh-tw.content:
「你知道今天天氣預報說什麼嗎？」
「說什麼？」
「說你出現的地方，有100%機率感覺特別暖。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 天氣很冷或很熱的時候都能用，通用款土味情話，對方多半會先白眼再笑
variations: 氣象局沒辦法預測你出現的時間，但每次你出現，天氣就變好了

en.content:
"Do you know what today's weather forecast says?"
"What does it say?"
"That wherever you show up, there's a 100% chance it suddenly feels warm."
en.editorNote: Works in any weather — a universally applicable cheesy line; expect an eye roll followed by a smile
variations: The weather app can't predict when you'll show up. But every time you do, the forecast improves.

zh-tw.content:
「你像一本書嗎？」
「哪種書？」
「那種翻了第一頁就放不下，到了最後一頁才發現時間不知道跑去哪了的書。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給愛讀書的對象最有加分效果，也適合搭配一本對方喜歡的書一起說
variations: 你是我想一直讀下去的那一種書——讀完了也不想還回去

en.content:
"Are you like a book?"
"What kind?"
"The kind where after the first page, I can't put you down — and when I finally look up, I don't know where the time went."
en.editorNote: Extra points if your partner loves to read — pair it with a book they mentioned wanting to try
variations: You're the kind of book I want to keep reading forever — and never return to the shelf.

zh-tw.content:
「你有沒有一首歌，聽著聽著就很想傳給某個人？」
「有啊。」
「我也有，而且不管聽什麼歌，第一個想傳的都是你。
還沒找到一首不想傳給你的。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給喜歡的人，搭配一首歌一起傳效果加倍，對方通常會問「是哪首歌」
variations: 你是我的單曲循環——不是因為我不想換，是因為我一直覺得你比任何歌都好聽

en.content:
"Do you ever hear a song and immediately want to send it to someone?"
"Yeah, sometimes."
"I have that too. Except no matter what song I hear, the first person I want to send it to is always you.
Haven't found one I don't want to share with you yet."
en.editorNote: Send this with a song attached for double the effect — they'll probably ask which one
variations: You're my song on repeat — not because I can't find another, but because nothing sounds better than you.

zh-tw.content:
「你知道你讓我困擾的地方是什麼嗎？」
「什麼？」
「就是你不在的時候，我連手機都覺得沒什麼好看的。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給習慣一直滑手機的對象，讓他知道你比手機更讓他在意
variations: 你是我關閉所有通知的理由——不是因為你打擾我，是因為有你才叫做真正的通知

en.content:
"Do you know what the most annoying thing about you is?"
"What?"
"When you're not around, I can't even find anything worth looking at on my phone."
en.editorNote: Send to someone who's always on their phone — it's a compliment and a gentle nudge at once
variations: You're the reason I turned off all my notifications — not because you bother me, but because you're the only alert that matters.

zh-tw.content:
「你知道我最討厭什麼天氣嗎？」
「什麼？」
「沒有你的天氣。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 簡短有力的經典款，不管什麼天氣都能用，對方第一反應通常是翻白眼，第二反應是笑
variations: 你是我的天氣預報——不管今天幾度，只要你在，就是晴天

en.content:
"Do you know what kind of weather I hate the most?"
"What?"
"The kind without you in it."
en.editorNote: Short and effective — works in any weather. Expect an eye roll, then a smile.
variations: You're my weather forecast — no matter what the temperature is, it's sunny when you're around.

zh-tw.content:
「你昨晚夢到什麼？」
「忘了。你呢？」
「我夢到你了。
然後早上醒來，我才發現不是夢——
因為我馬上就想要傳訊息給你了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 早上傳效果最好，讓對方一起床就有好心情，曖昧期特別好用
variations: 就算你沒在我夢裡，我一睜開眼第一個想到的還是你——夢不夢到已經不重要了

en.content:
"What did you dream about last night?"
"I forgot. You?"
"I dreamed about you.
Then I woke up and realized it wasn't a dream —
because the first thing I wanted to do was message you."
en.editorNote: Send in the morning for maximum effect — starts their day on a warm note. Great for the flirty phase.
variations: Even if you don't show up in my dreams, you're still the first thing I think of when I open my eyes — being in the dream doesn't even matter.

zh-tw.content:
「你知道你跟春天有什麼共同點嗎？」
「什麼？」
「你一出現，我就覺得冬天終於過了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 換季的時候說效果特別好，也適合在對方心情不好、剛走出低潮的時候傳
variations: 你比春天更準時——春天有時候會遲，但你出現的那一刻，我立刻就暖了

en.content:
"Do you know what you have in common with spring?"
"What?"
"Every time you show up, I feel like winter finally ended."
en.editorNote: Perfect at the start of a new season, or when your person is coming back from a rough patch — simple and warm
variations: You're more reliable than spring — spring sometimes runs late, but you? The warmth hits the moment you arrive.

zh-tw.content:
「你是火鍋嗎？」
「為什麼？」
「因為天冷的時候，我第一個想到的就是你。
然後就覺得暖了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 冬天或天氣變冷的時候傳，配上一張火鍋的照片效果加倍，不做作又暖心
variations: 你是薑母鴨嗎？因為有你在，我連最冷的夜都覺得可以撐過去

en.content:
"Are you a bowl of hot pot?"
"Why?"
"Because when the weather gets cold, you're the first thing I think of.
And then I'm warm."
en.editorNote: Perfect in winter or on a rainy day — pair with a photo of hot pot for extra effect. Cozy, not overdone.
variations: Are you a warm cup of tea? Because just knowing you exist makes the coldest nights feel manageable.

zh-tw.content:
「你知道你讓我特別安心的地方是什麼嗎？」
「什麼？」
「就是不管我今天過得有多糟，只要你的名字出現在螢幕上，我就自動好了一半。
我也不知道為什麼，但已經習慣了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給讓你安心的人，讓他知道他對你來說有多重要，比說「我喜歡你」更日常、更有感
variations: 你的訊息是我一天裡最不需要想太多的事——看到了就開心，不需要理由

en.content:
"Do you know what it is about you that makes me feel calm?"
"What?"
"No matter how rough my day is, the moment your name shows up on my screen, I'm already halfway okay.
I don't fully understand it. But I've gotten used to it."
en.editorNote: Send to the person who makes you feel settled — it says more than 'I like you' without being too much
variations: Your messages are the one thing in my day that I don't have to think about. I see your name. I'm happy. No explanation needed.

zh-tw.content:
「你知道你讓我想到什麼嗎？」
「什麼？」
「那種咖啡廳裡偶爾會遇到的貓。
牠不理你，但如果牠突然多看你一眼，
你就覺得這一天值了。
你就是那種程度的存在。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個你默默很在乎、但不太確定對方感覺的人，帶點俏皮但真心
variations: 換個說法：你是讓我無論如何都想靠近、但靠近了又不知道說什麼的那一種人

en.content:
"Do you know what you remind me of?"
"What?"
"That cat you sometimes find in a coffee shop.
It doesn't acknowledge you, but if it glances your way — just once —
your whole day feels worth it.
You're exactly that kind of presence."
en.editorNote: Send to someone you care about quietly — playful but sincere, great for the uncertain-feelings phase
variations: Another angle: you're the person I always want to get closer to, and then when I do, I don't know what to say.

zh-tw.content:
「你知道你跟雨傘有什麼共同點嗎？」
「什麼？」
「最需要的時候，我第一個想到的就是你。
而且只要你在，我就覺得今天不管下什麼，都沒有那麼嚴重了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 下雨天傳效果最好，讓對方感覺自己是你的避風港，不做作又暖心
variations: 你是我的備案，也是我的第一選擇——不一樣的地方在於，備案是退而求其次，你從來不是。

en.content:
"Do you know what you have in common with an umbrella?"
"What?"
"When I need one most, you're the first thing I reach for.
And with you around, whatever the day throws at me doesn't feel so serious anymore."
en.editorNote: Perfect to send on a rainy day — tells them they're your shelter without being dramatic
variations: You're both my backup plan and my first choice — the difference being, a backup is a compromise. You never are.

zh-tw.content:
「你知道我記憶力很不好嗎？」
「真的嗎？」
「對，會忘東忘西。但你說的話，我一句都沒忘過。
我也不知道為什麼，就是記得特別牢。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那個說過你不記得細節的另一半，讓他知道你其實有在聽，只是選擇性地留住了最重要的那些
variations: 你說的每一句話，有沒有覺得我記得比你想的多？不是故意的，是你說的太值得記住了。

en.content:
"You know I have a terrible memory, right?"
"Really?"
"Yeah, I forget things all the time. But I haven't forgotten a single thing you've ever said to me.
I'm not sure why. They just stuck."
en.editorNote: Send to a partner who thinks you never listen — shows them you do, just selectively remember what matters most
variations: Have you noticed I remember more of what you've said than you'd expect? It's not intentional. What you say is just worth holding onto.

zh-tw.content:
「你喜歡吃湯圓嗎？」
「還好啊，為什麼？」
「因為有你在，圓滿這件事我早就懂了。
湯圓只是提醒我一下，原來這種感覺早就有個名字。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 冬至或元宵節傳效果最好，也適合配上一張湯圓照片一起傳，節慶感十足又不失溫度
variations: 你是我的湯圓——甜的，圓的，而且不管什麼時候出現，都讓這個節日多了意義。

en.content:
"Do you like sweet rice balls?"
"They're okay — why?"
"Because with you around, I already know what completeness feels like.
The rice balls just reminded me there's been a name for it all along."
en.editorNote: Perfect around Lantern Festival or Winter Solstice — send with a photo of the food for full seasonal effect
variations: You're my sweet rice ball — round, sweet, and no matter when you show up, the day suddenly has more meaning.

zh-tw.content:
「你知道化學裡的催化劑是什麼嗎？」
「讓反應更快發生的東西？」
「對，而且它自己不會消耗掉，但沒有它，很多事情都不會開始。
你對我來說，就是那種存在。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 理科背景的對象看到這個會先確認定義正確，然後才意識到是情話，時間差讓它加倍有趣
variations: 你是我生活反應的催化劑——不是主角，但如果沒有你，什麼都不會發生得那麼順。

en.content:
"Do you know what a catalyst is in chemistry?"
"Something that makes reactions happen faster?"
"Right. And it doesn't get used up itself — but without it, a lot of things would never start.
That's exactly what you are to me."
en.editorNote: A science-minded partner will first verify the chemistry definition, then slowly realize it's a pickup line — the delayed reaction makes it funnier
variations: You're the catalyst in my life's reactions — not the main element, but without you, nothing happens as smoothly.
